title: Maestro Backlog
description: A running queue of valid findings the orchestrator chose NOT to fix in the originating work stream. Backlog items never re-enter an active task automatically — each becomes a standalone future task the human may launch on demand.

id.description: Stable backlog id. Reusable as a task_id when the human launches a run against it.

finding.description: The full normalized finding text — enough that a future run can re-scope it without the original context.

acceptance.description: What resolution looks like, carried forward so the future task has a clear target.

decided_by.description: Usually orchestrator; records who chose to backlog rather than block.

rationale.description: Why this was backlogged instead of blocked — the audit answer to 'why didn't we fix this now?'

date.description: ISO-8601 date the item was backlogged.
